fact: December 27 is exactly 361 days into the year, which means only 4 days are left until the next year starts!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when snow crystals form the most perfect six-sided shapes because the air is super cold and dry.
Polar bears in the Arctic are at their fluffiest right now - their winter coats are so thick they can sleep comfortably on ice that's -40°F!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States, making December 27 one of the darkest days for after-school activities.
December 27 falls during Capricorn season, which started on December 22 - people born on this day are supposedly as stubborn as mountain goats!
Tree sap in cold regions is basically frozen solid right now, which is why you never see maple syrup being made in winter.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, December 27 is peak summer - cicadas are buzzing so loudly they can drown out conversations!
The moon looks extra bright and crisp on winter nights like this because cold air holds less water vapor to blur your view.
Penguins in Antarctica are in the middle of their breeding season right now, huddling together in groups of thousands to stay warm.
December 27 is exactly 5 days after the winter solstice, but the sun is still rising later each morning in most places - the latest sunrise is yet to come!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are working overtime right now, using their waxy needles to photosynthesize even in freezing temperatures.
The air is so dry in many cold places on December 27 that static electricity builds up like crazy - you can shock someone just by touching them!
